blocks.content: Sophomore year found its pace faster than freshman year had. They knew the schools now, knew the distance, knew which nights were for phone calls and which were just for texts. The rhythm was theirs.
But something was different in Kit this year. Darius noticed first.
Kit didn't answer. Darius had learned to read that as a yes.
Kit leaned back in his chair. Looked at the ceiling.

blocks.speaker: Darius
blocks.content: How long have you been working it out.

blocks.speaker: Darius
blocks.content: Is this a feelings thing or a logistics thing.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: The feelings have been sorted since we were twelve. It's the how.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I need it to mean something. Something real. Not a restaurant and a ring box. Something she'll remember the way I'll remember it.
Darius looked at him for a moment.
Kit looked at him. It was the simplest thing anyone had said to him in months. He didn't know why he hadn't gotten there himself.
